mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next month when the policy rate is in a clear downtrend.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX forward returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines over the prior 3 months, or if the sign of the relation turns negative in any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still elevated, markets discount further tightening and equity valuations compress, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL rising over the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not underperform untriggered periods over 20 trading days, or if positive SPX returns occur in a majority of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger retail demand and better near-term revenue momentum, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are not higher after positive RSAFS trend periods, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An upward break in UNRATE signals weakening labor income and higher recession risk, so equities should decline over the next week as investors reprice cyclical earnings.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if SPX does not fall over 5 trading days after the unemployment trigger more often than not, or if the effect disappears in consecutive late-cycle slowdowns.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in PAYEMS and INDPRO indicate broad labor and industrial weakening, which depresses earnings expectations and should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint PAYEMS and INDPRO weakness does not predict lower 20-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect fully and the interaction adds no incremental power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO from a low base signals improving supply-side throughput and stronger cyclical earnings, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns do not improve after positive INDPRO trends, or if the relationship is absent in early-cycle recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after a prior contraction signals housing demand stabilization and downstream consumption support, which should lift equities over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its prior 6-month average after a decline
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST rebounds are not followed by higher 60-day SPX returns, or if the signal fails specifically after prior housing contractions.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive while UNRATE is falling, the economy is expanding without labor deterioration, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next month; the signal fails when output growth is accompanied by rising unemployment.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-and-unemployment condition does not outperform baseline over 20 trading days, or if positive SPX returns remain common when GDPC1 expands but UNRATE rises.
